body:
tier=smart harness= model= probe=no viable provider
no viable harness found
execute-loop: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found

Bead agent-706244af requires changes in the DDx CLI repo, not this agent library repo.

What is done
------------
- Confirmed this worktree is github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent (go.mod line 1).
- Confirmed the error signature "execute-loop: all tiers exhausted - no viable provider found" is not produced anywhere in this repository. The only "all tiers exhausted" text lives in internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_cache.go and an unrelated test (service_route_attempts_test.go), neither of which emits the bead's error string.
- Confirmed "execute-loop" appears only in internal/core/loop_test.go (a different concept — the agent reasoning/tool loop, not DDx's queue execution loop) and documentation. The execute-loop tier fallback that emits the bead's failure is implemented by the DDx CLI, not this library.
- The bead description's in-scope list explicitly names /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/cmd and /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent, and the labels include repo:ddx. The DDx CLI source tree is not present in this worktree.

What is blocking
----------------
- Wrong worktree / wrong repo. The bead was queued against the agent repo's bead database but targets code in a sibling repository (ddx/cli) that this execution cannot reach or modify.
- Out-of-scope guard in the bead itself: "Agent library Claude alias normalization; fixed and released in agent v0.9.8." Anything we would plausibly change here (profile resolution, route-status population, harness dispatch) is agent-library code and therefore out of scope per the bead.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
- Re-route the bead to the DDx CLI repo's bead queue (labels repo:ddx already indicate this), or run execute-bead from a worktree rooted at /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli.
- Once in the CLI repo, reproduce: `ddx work --profile smart --once` fails pre-execution with "execute-loop: all tiers exhausted - no viable provider found" while `ddx agent run --profile smart ...` (direct CLI) succeeds. The divergence is almost certainly in how the server worker constructs its agent.Service / ServiceConfig (profile list, candidates, workDir) vs. how the standalone `ddx agent run` path wires it. Likely candidates: worker does not propagate project config / ~/.ddx/config when spawning the execute-loop, or it constructs a Service without ModelRoutes populated, so sc.ModelRouteNames() returns empty and RouteStatus returns {} exactly as observed.
- Regression test belongs in the ddx CLI repo (fake viable harness/service response, assert execute-loop reaches execute-bead).

Nothing to commit from this worktree.
